segments.text: My spirit is crushed; my life is extinguished; the grave is ready for me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Mockers surround me. I see how bitterly they ridicule me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God, you need to put down a pledge for me with yourself, for who else will be my guarantor?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You have closed their minds to understanding, [^95] so do not let them win!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They betray friends to gain benefit for themselves and their children suffer for it. [^96]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He has made me a proverb of ridicule[^97] among the people; they spit in my face.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My eyes are worn out from crying and my body is a shadow of its former self.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People who think they are good are shocked to see me. Those who are innocent are troubled by the godless. [^98]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who are right keep going, and those whose hands are clean grow stronger and stronger.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why don't you come back and repeat again what you've been saying?—yet I still won't find a wise man among you!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My life is over. My plans are gone. My heart is broken.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They turn night into day, and say that daylight is close to darkness. [^99]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What am I looking for? To make my home in Sheol, [^100] to make my bed in darkness?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Should I call the grave[^101] my father, and the maggot my mother or my sister?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So then where is my hope? Can anyone see any hope for me?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Will hope go down with me to the gates of Sheol? Will we go down together into the dust?
segments.speaker: Unknown
